checks.command: 3 registry/ files + root NIZAM.json exist, no stray files
every path/authoritative_source/key_documents/schemas/templates entry in NIZAM.json resolves on disk (71 paths checked)
NIZAM.json carries semver version 0.1.0 matching the framework version
NIZAM.json indexes exactly 6 modules, 7 schemas, 8 templates, 13 capabilities, plus self-reference
scope_definition_patterns.md and README.md have frontmatter with 6 required keys
no 'nizamiq' branding in registry/ or NIZAM.json (file-count guarded)
registry/ contains exactly the 3 contracted files, no stray extras

adversarial_check.description: Two-part check beyond the 11 listed commands. (1) Context-router exercise per DD-1 intent: randomly sampled 3 of 13 capabilities (seed=42: nizam-governance-skill-manifest, governance-inheritance-protocol, nizam-documentation-standard), resolved each authoritative_source on disk, and verified semantic consistency -- for the two markdown-backed capabilities, the resolved document's frontmatter 'id' matches the capability id exactly and its 'title' shares meaningful vocabulary with the capability summary; for the JSON-backed capability (tools/skill.json), the resolved 'description' field shares 8 meaningful words with the capability summary (agnostic/capability/manifest/payload/runtime/single/skill/unified). All 3 resolve to the correct, semantically-matching document -- no index entry points at the wrong file. (2) Schema negative test: copied NIZAM.json to the scratchpad, mutated only 'framework.version' to the non-semver string 'not-a-semver', and re-validated the mutated copy against the real registry/nizam-index.schema.json -- rejected, with the validator error specifically attributing rejection to the 'pattern' constraint on the 'framework.version' path (not an incidental unrelated error), confirming the semver pattern is load-bearing. The real repo's NIZAM.json (framework.version=0.1.0) was independently re-confirmed unmutated after the test.
